cards.back: A game where two or more players make decisions repeatedly over time, with the goal of maximizing their payoff.
A concept in game theory that describes a situation where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged.
A classic example of a repeated game, where two prisoners must decide whether to cooperate or defect, with the outcome depending on the actions of the other prisoner.
The study of repeated games, which aims to understand how players make decisions over time to maximize their payoff, and how these decisions affect the outcome of the game.
A type of repeated game where the players have an incentive to keep playing, as the expected payoff is higher than the cost, and the game continues indefinitely until it collapses.
